intro: Barosma betulina, also known as the "round-leaved barosma," is a plant native to the mountainous regions of South Africa. The resin extracted from this plant has been traditionally used in traditional medicine and as an insect repellent due to its strong, pungent aroma and potential antimicrobial properties. However, further research is needed to fully understand the benefits and potential risks of using Barosma betulina resin as a medicinal or insect-repellent agent.
